name: Miami (United States)

colors.symbolism: the orange groves and the Florida sun
the verdant landscape and palms of South Florida
the central seal of the City of Miami

description: A field divided diagonally from the upper hoist to the lower fly: orange in the upper-fly half and green in the lower-hoist half, with the white circular seal of the City of Miami at the centre.
history: Designed by Edward S. Hartley and adopted by the City of Miami in 1933.

features.symbolism: Orange for the citrus and sunshine, green for the lush vegetation of South Florida; the diagonal recalls the city's location on the southern tip of the Florida peninsula.

features.role: Seal of the City of Miami
features.symbolism: The seal depicts a sun rising over a palm tree, with rays radiating outward, encircled by the city's name and the year of its incorporation (1896); represents the city's tropical location and its founding.
